chunks.text: In my town, an old Western style house has remained.
but one year the townspeople decided that it would be restored.
The house had been constructed about eighty years ago.
After the war it was abandoned for a long time.
Its roof had crumbled, and the garden was covered with weeds.
Citizens raised their voices, saying this building should be preserved as part of our heritage.
The house was recognized as something historical.
Finally a budget was established by the city, and the house was gradually repaired.
The roof was redone, the walls were painted, and the windows were replaced.
On the day it opened, I stepped inside.
In its old pillars, the past has been carved.
An old building was made to contribute new survival by the hands of its people.
